title: sarcozona pulleinei
intro: Sarcozona pulleinei, also known as the red sandpaper bush, is a shrub native to Australia with a unique, sandpapery texture to its leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in Western Australia for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Sarcozona pulleinei include treating wounds, skin irritations, and minor cuts, as well as reducing fever and alleviating coughs. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to be used topically or internally for its medicinal properties.
